rulings.Case Title: MARTHA OROZCO VS. KING XIONG CHONG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 24, 2024 line 4. DEFENDANT KING CHONG, MAGGIE HO DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurer to the cause of action for trespass is sustained with leave to amend on uncertainty grounds for Plaintiff to allege what acts of the defendants constitute trespass and the timeframe of the trespass. It is unclear from the complaint if P alleges that Defendants have been trespassing since the date of fire or since some later date; whether Plaintiff claims that defendants commit trespass by withholding possession; by entering for remodeling or by some other acts. The Court does not reach the issue of the sufficiency of allegations at this time. Demurrer to the cause of action for fraud is sustained with leave to amend on failure to state facts and uncertainty grounds. It is unclear whether Plaintiff assets fraud based on misrepresentation or fraud based on concealment or both. To the extent Plaintiff attempts to allege fraud based on misrepresentation, the elements are not alleged with requisite particularity. To the extent Plaintiff attempts to allege fraud based on concealment, no facts are alleged to show that Plaintiff "would not have acted as [s]he did if [a]he had known of the concealed or suppressed fact."Demurer is otherwise overruled. All other causes of action subject to this demurrer are sufficiently stated and are not uncertain. Declaration of Plaintiff's counsel is stricken as not appropriate on a demurrer. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ROBERT COSGAYA VS. MARIA CLAUDIO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 24, 2024 line 5. DEFENDANT MARIA CLAUDIO, ANTHONY BOSCH DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Action CUD-22-668573 has been dismissed. Current complaint states a cause of action for unlawful detainer per CC 789 ("A tenancy or other estate at will, however created, may be terminated by the landlord's giving notice in writing to the tenant, in the manner prescribed by Section 1162 of the Code of Civil Procedure, to remove from the premises within a period of not less than 30 days, to be specified in the notice"); includes allegations of exemption and the reason thereof from TPA 2019 and SFRO; and is not uncertain. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: CYNTHIA AVILES-LANDAU VS. VICTORIA MCBEE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 24, 2024 line 6. PLAINTIFF CYNTHIA AVILES-LANDAU , ADMINISTRATOR OF THE THELMA B. AVILES ESTATE MOTION TO SET ASIDE DISMISSAL is DENIED without prejudice. Plaintiff moves to set aside the dismissal based on "inadvertence." The motion is supported by Lee Declaration stating in relevant part: "My reading of the settlement agreement was that the dismissal was required by the settlement agreement, but that based on the stipulation the Court had signed along with both parties the court would retain jurisdiction subject to paragraph "u."" In the supplemental declaration, Mr. Lee admits that there is no court signature retaining jurisdiction in the Stipulation. Mr. Lee points to a part of the settlement agreement titled "Reviewed and Approved" that is clearly crossed out and the signature and date line is left blank. The only sections that are filled out (albeit are also crossed out) are the Judge's name and "[Print Name]" section that does not match the judge's name. It is unclear how this largely blank crossed out section, missing any signature or date, could have been mistaken for a Court Order. It is also not clear why the action was dismissed on June 6, 2024 if Plaintiff believed that "completely and without a doubt, the breach was as of May 15, 2024." The settlement agreement provides: "plaintiff shall file a dismissal with prejudiced,, provided that defendant complied with all other terms contained herein, and defendant shall prepare and present all proper and necessary documents, including said dismissal, for plaintiff's signature and shall be responsible for filing and service thereof." The dismissal was prepared, filed and served by Plaintiff on June 6, 2024 (See Dismissal and Notice of Entry of Dismissal filed on June 6, 2024), 3 weeks after Plaintiff "completely and without a doubt" believed that Defendant was in breach of the stipulation. Mr. Lee does not explain why he believed that the settlement agreement required a dismissal under these circumstances. Based on the declarations before the Court there is no basis to find mistake, surprise or excusable neglect. The dismissal language of the stipulation is clear and unambiguous as to the triggering event for Plaintiff's obligation to dismiss; Plaintiff strongly believed that Defendant failed to comply with the settlement agreement three weeks before the dismissal; the part of the agreement purportedly containing a judge's signature is clearly crossed out. On October 1, 2024 the Court requested supplemental declaration addressing 2 narrow issues. Supplemental declaration filed by Defendant contains 31 pages largely unresponsive to the Court's request. Therefore, paragraphs 5-15 of the Supplemental Declaration and all the exhibits are stricken. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
